elements.sent-2411-001: In a measure space with a very mild structure, a {\it good grid , we introduced a scale of Besov Banach spaces of {\it distributions with negative smoothness.}}
elements.sent-2411-002: We establish an atomic decomposition in terms of Dirac masses and Dipoles, that is, pairs of Diracs masses with equal charge but opposite signal.
elements.sent-2411-003: Atomic decomposition is a well established idea in harmonic analysis.
elements.sent-2411-004: Literature abounds with numerous techniques to express various types of spaces using a simpler subclass of its elements, usually called atoms.
elements.sent-2411-005: For example, we can mention the decomposition of Besov Spaces by de Souza \cite{souza-atomicdec}\cite{souza2 , Frazier and Jawerth \cite{frazier , as well Girardi and Sweldens \cite{Sweldens1997 , which offers a decomposition of the Lebesgue spaces $L^r$, with $r > 1$, of a measure space with mild additional structure (a grid) in terms of unbalanced Haar wavelets.}}}
elements.sent-2411-006: An atomic decomposition for a Banach space enhance the toolkit available to study it.
elements.sent-2411-007: This paper works with measures space with a good grids.
elements.sent-2411-008: A measure space with a good grid is a probability space $(I, \mathcal{A , \mu)$ endowed with a nested sequence of finite $\mathcal{A}$-measurable partitions (up to zero measure sets) $\mathcal{P = (\mathcal{P ^k)_{k\in \mathbb{N }$ such that.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-010: There exists $\lambda, \lambda_* \in (0,1)$ such that, if $Q\subset P$ \begin{equation}\lambda_* \leq \dfrac{|Q|}{|P|} \leq \lambda.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-012: We denote by $\Omega_P$ the set of all children of $P$.
elements.sent-2411-013: If $\mathcal{P}$ is a good grid, then \begin{equation}\label{def-cgr} C_{GR}:=\sup_{P \in \mathcal{P}} \# \Omega_P < \infty.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-014: This result is part of a trend of developing harmonic analysis on phase spaces with low regularity.. In S.
elements.sent-2411-015: A key ingredient will be dyadic harmonic analysis (see Pereya \cite{dya2}\cite{pereyra and also L\'opez-S\'anchez, Martell and Parcet \cite{dya ), particulary with the use of unbalanced Haar wavelets as constructed by Girardi and Sweldens \cite{Sweldens1997 .}}}
elements.sent-2411-016: In a measure space with a good grid $I$ we use unbalanced Haar wavelets to define Banach spaces to define a scale of Banach spaces $\vs$ and $\bs \subset (\vs)^\star$.
elements.sent-2411-017: Those are analogous to the corresponding Besov spaces on $\mathbb{R ^n$.}
elements.sent-2411-018: Indeed \begin{mainthm There is a pseudo-metric $d$ on $I$ such that the space $\vs$, with $0< s< 1$, is the space of $s$-H\"older continuous functions with respect to $d$.}
elements.sent-2411-019: Those Dirac masses are analogous to Dirac measures.
elements.sent-2411-020: We define the space $PS^s$ of $s$-particle systems as limits on $(\vs)^\star$ of finite combinations of Dirac masses.
elements.sent-2411-021: It turns out that \begin{mainthm There is a unconditional Schauder basis of $B^{-s _{1,1}$ that contains only dipoles and a single Dirac mass.}}
elements.sent-2411-023: Girardi and Sweldens \cite{Sweldens1997 constructed a unconditional basis of $L^p$, for $1< p< \infty$, associated to a sequence of partitions on a measure space, similar to the classical Haar basis associated to the sequence of dyadic partition of $[0,1]$.}
elements.sent-2411-025: Let \begin{equation}H_Q = \bigcup\limits_{j=0}^\infty H_{Q,j},\end{equation} be a collection of pairs $(A,B)$, where $A\subset \Omega_Q$, $B\subset \Omega_Q$, and $\cup A$ and $\cup B$ are disjoint, such that $\mathcal{H _{Q,j}$ are defined recursively in the following way.}
elements.sent-2411-026: Choose an total order \begin{equation}P_1^Q \preceq P_1^Q \preceq \cdots \preceq P_{n_Q}^Q\end{equation} of $\Omega_Q$ and define \begin{equation}H_{Q,0}=(\{P_1^Q,\ldots, P^{Q}_{[n_Q/2]}\}, \{P^{Q}_{[n_Q/2]+1}, P^{Q}_{n_Q}\})\end{equation} and for every pair \begin{equation}(S_1,S_2) = (\{S_1^1, \ldots, S_{n_1}^1\},\{S_1^2, \ldots, S_{n_2}^2\}) \in H_{Q,j},\end{equation} where \begin{equation}S_1^1 \preceq \cdots \preceq S^1_{n_1} \preceq S^2_1 \preceq \cdots \preceq S_{n_2}^2,\end{equation} we add the pairs \begin{equation}{ccc} (\{S_1^1,\ldots,S^1_{[n_1/2]}\},\{S^1_{[n_1/2]+1},\ldots, S_{n_1}^1\}) (\{S_1^2,\ldots,S^2_{[n_2/2]}\},\{S^2_{[n_2/2]+1},\ldots, S_{n_2}^2\}) \end{equation} to $\mathcal{H _{Q,{j+1 }$.}}
elements.sent-2411-027: Since the $\mathcal{P}$ is a good grid, there are only finitely many $j\in \mathbb{N}$ for which $\mathcal{H _{Q,j}\neq \emptyset$.}

elements.section-2411-005: Dirac Masses, Particle Systems and Dipoles
elements.sent-2411-070: In this section, we prove the elements of $\bs$ can be described by a combination of distributions similar to the usual Dirac masses.
elements.sent-2411-071: Since $\vs$-observables are defined almost everywhere, the usual definition of Dirac distributions does not make sense, since the evaluation at a point is meaningless.
elements.sent-2411-072: We will write $x\in Q_j$ for every $j$.
elements.sent-2411-073: Define the {\it Dirac mass $\delta_x$ associated to $x\in \hat{I}$ as \begin{equation}\label{diracs-formula} \delta_x = \lim\limits_{j \to \infty} \dfrac{{{1}}_{Q_j}}{|Q_j|},\end{equation} where the limit is with respect to the norm on $\bs$.}
elements.sent-2411-074: Note that $\delta_x$ is well-defined due to the following lemma.
elements.sent-2411-075: With this metric $\hat{I}$ is a Cantor set.

elements.sent-2411-078: It is enough to show that $(\phi_j)_{j \geq 0}$ is a Cauchy sequence.. Indeed, as \begin{equation}\phi_{j+1} = \phi_j + \sum\limits_{\substack{(P_1,P_{2}) \in H_{Q_j} Q_{j+1}\in P_1\cup P_{2}}} \dfrac{|Q_{j+1} \cap P_1|\cdot |P_1|+|Q_{j+1} \cap P_2|\cdot |P_2|}{|P_1 \cup P_2|} \phi_{(P_1,P_2)}.\end{equation}.

elements.sent-2411-082: Next we define the class of distributions that will be the building blocks of the atomic decomposition of $\bs$.

elements.sent-2411-085: An useful example of particle system that we are considering are the following: \end{defi \begin{defi A linear functional $\gamma: \vs \to \mathbb{C}$ is called a dipole if \begin{equation}\gamma = \delta_x - \delta_y,\end{equation} for some $x,y \in \hat{I}$ with $x\neq y$.. \end{defi.}}}

elements.sent-2411-097: By assumption, the Diracs agree up to the level $N$ so, by \eqref{dirac-nonbinary-expression , \begin{equation}\begin{array}{cc} \delta_{x_{Q_1}} - \delta_{x_{Q_2}} = \displaystyle\sum\limits_{k=N}^{\infty} \sum\limits_{\substack{(P_1,P_2) \in \mathcal{H}_{Q_k^1} Q_{k+1}^1\in P_1 \cup P_2}} \dfrac{|Q_{k+1}^1 \cap P_1|\cdot |P_1|+|Q_{k+1}^1 \cap P_2|\cdot |P_2|}{|P_1 \cup P_2|} \phi_{(P_1,P_2)} + \displaystyle\sum\limits_{k=N}^{\infty} \sum\limits_{\substack{(P_1,P_2) \in \mathcal{H}_{Q_k^2} Q_{k+1}^2\in P_1 \cup P_2}} \dfrac{|Q_{k+1}^2 \cap P_1|\cdot |P_1|+|Q_{k+1}^2 \cap P_2|\cdot |P_2|}{|P_1 \cup P_2|} \phi_{(P_1,P_2)} \end{array}\end{equation} so we can compute the norm \begin{equation}\label{termAdef} \begin{array}{cccc} |\delta_{x_{Q_1}} - \delta_{x_{Q_2}}|_\bs \leq \displaystyle\sum\limits_{k=N}^{\infty} |Q_k^1|^s \sum\limits_{\substack{(P_1,P_2) \in \mathcal{H}_{Q_k^1} Q_{k+1}^1\in P_1 \cup P_2}} \dfrac{|Q_{k+1}^1 \cap P_1|\cdot |P_1|+|Q_{k+1}^1 \cap P_2|\cdot |P_2|}{|P_1 \cup P_2|} + \displaystyle\sum\limits_{k=N}^{\infty} |Q_k^2|^s \sum\limits_{\substack{(P_1,P_2) \in \mathcal{H}_{Q_k^2} Q_{k+1}^2\in P_1 \cup P_2}} \dfrac{|Q_{k+1}^2 \cap P_1|\cdot |P_1|+|Q_{k+1}^2 \cap P_2|\cdot |P_2|}{|P_1 \cup P_2|}. \end{array}\end{equation} Now, for any $k\geq N$, since $\mathcal{P}$ is a good grid, we have \begin{equation}|Q_k^i|=|Q_N^i|\cdot \prod\limits_{m=N+1}^k \dfrac{|Q_m^i|}{|Q_{m-1}^i|}\leq \lambda^{k-N} \cdot |Q_N^i| = \lambda^{k-N} \cdot |Q|\end{equation} Hence, \begin{equation}{llll} |\delta_{x_{Q_1}} - \delta_{x_{Q_2}}|_\bs \leq C_{GR} \left[ \displaystyle\sum\limits_{k=N}^\infty |Q_k^1|^s + \sum\limits_{k=N}^\infty |Q_k^2|^s \right] \leq 2 C_{GR} \left[\displaystyle\sum\limits_{k=N}^\infty \lambda^{s(k-N)}\cdot |Q|^s \right] \leq 2 C_{GR} \left[\lambda^{-Ns} \displaystyle\sum\limits_{k=N}^\infty \lambda^{ks} \cdot |Q|^s \right] = 2 C_{GR} \left[\lambda^{-Ns} \dfrac{\lambda^{Ns}}{1-\lambda^s} \cdot |Q|^s \right] = \dfrac{2 C_{GR}}{1-\lambda^s} \cdot |Q|^s. \end{equation} as desired.}
